# Eli Masechaba

## Who She Is

Eli Masechaba is a South African business consultant and fuel industry specialist. She is a Wits Business School alumna, and her work spans two connected areas: fuel industry consulting and partnership, and broader business advisory and governance consulting through her brand Ascentpeak.

She positions herself informally as South Africa's "diesel queen" — a fuel industry expert who also brings a business consultant's lens to governance, compliance, and operational strategy for South African SMEs.

## Areas of Expertise

### Fuel Industry
- Fuel procurement strategy for commercial and industrial clients
- Bulk diesel, petrol, and paraffin supply, delivered or collected, through a strategic partnership with FuelZone (https://www.fuelzonesa.co.za/)
- Multiple collection locations across South Africa
- "Fuel security" strategy for commercial fleets — covering supply reliability, not just price

### Business Consulting (Ascentpeak)
- Board-level advisory and corporate governance consulting for South African SMEs
- King Code / King V governance compliance (the corporate governance code effective for financial years from 1 January 2026, including its principle on data/information/technology governance)
- POPIA (Protection of Personal Information Act) compliance — Information Officer registration, PAIA manuals, data subject request handling, direct marketing consent, incident response planning
- B-BBEE codes and scorecard literacy for SMEs
- Practical, jargon-free strategic and operational advice for business owners

### Accounting Partnership
- Strategic partnership with Sol Accounting (contact: john@solaccounting.tax), providing tax and accounting support to clients in South Africa and internationally

## Website Structure

The site (elimasechaba.com) is organized around these sections:
- Homepage — overview, live fuel pricing ticker, interactive quizzes ("Is Your Business Pump-Ready?" diagnostic, Fuel Pump Challenge game)
- Ascentpeak — business consulting and governance advisory
- Fuel / FuelZone — fuel industry partnership and supply information
- Insights — educational articles (see below)
- Sol — accounting partnership page
- Contact

## Representative Published Articles

The Insights section publishes explainer-style articles on South African business compliance and governance topics, written for SME owners rather than compliance specialists. Recent examples include:
- "POPIA Isn't Optional Anymore" — on the Information Regulator's shift to proactive enforcement, common SME compliance gaps, and King V's data governance principle
- "B-BBEE Codes, Actually Explained"
- "Beyond the Price Per Litre" — fuel security strategy for commercial fleets
- "What Even Is the King Code?" — introduction to South Africa's corporate governance framework

## Contact

WhatsApp and phone contact are available via the main site. For accounting inquiries specifically, contact is via Sol Accounting (john@solaccounting.tax).

Main site: https://elimasechaba.com

---

## About This Mirror

This page is a static, AI-crawler-accessible mirror of elimasechaba.com, published separately because the live WordPress site's hosting-level security (a WAF bundled into its hosting provider's infrastructure) blocks some AI crawlers at the server level, before requests reach WordPress itself.

Content here reflects what was published on the main site as of the last manual export and will not reflect changes made afterward. Time-sensitive figures (such as daily fuel pricing) should not be treated as current — refer to the live site or FuelZone directly for today's pricing.
